Current yaw is -120 degrees (after five left turns of 30 degrees each). The bearing is -53 degrees, meaning the goal is now 67 degrees to the right of my current heading.

Wait - this is confusing. If my yaw is -120 degrees and the bearing is -53 degrees, that means the goal is actually to my RIGHT (since -53 is greater than -120). I've been turning left when I should be turning right!

The bearing sign convention seems to be: negative values mean the goal is to the left of my heading, positive values mean to the right. But if my yaw is -120 and bearing is -53, the goal is at -53 degrees relative to world coordinates, while I'm facing -120 degrees. So the goal is 67 degrees to my RIGHT.

I need to turn RIGHT to face the goal.
